name: IRC_CAND_MSG_STORE
description: This table stores a dump of the email and SMS content  as it is used to process newly received emails and SMS.

columns.Name: CAND_MSG_STORE_ID
columns.Comments: Primary key for the IRC_CAND_MSG_STORE table.

columns.Name: MSG_TYPE
columns.Comments: This is a discriminator column .possible values will be ORA_EMAIL/ORA_SMS

columns.Name: PROCESSING_STATUS
columns.Comments:
Processing status of the record.'ORA_NEW' when the message dump row is created (default value).
'ORA_PROCESSED' when the processing is successful.'ORA_FAILED' if the processing fails even after retries.

columns.Name: REFERENCE_ID
columns.Comments:
Store INTERACTION_ID in case of SMS.
CAND_MSG_ID in case of Email (Optional).

columns.Name: RETRY_COUNT
columns.Comments: How many times the batch job has tried to process the record.

columns.Name: PROCESSING_ERROR_MESSAGE
columns.Comments: Error message while processing the row

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.
